Trend Scout Daily Market Intelligence — 2026-04-22

Sources fetched at 2026-04-22 01:00 UTC:

  • Trendshift: https://trendshift.io — repository engagement page dated 2026-04-21
  • TrustMRR: https://trustmrr.com — verified startup revenue leaderboard

Executive read

Today is an AI-agent infrastructure + creator/business automation day. Trendshift is dominated by open-source agent frameworks, skills packs, context tooling, monitoring dashboards, and AI video/content generation. TrustMRR is dominated by businesses selling direct ROI: creator commerce, healthcare/weight loss, resumes/jobs, ads attribution, SEO/AEO, AI sales agents, video generation, and LinkedIn/social automation.

There is no exact name overlap between the top Trendshift repos and top TrustMRR startups in the fetched lists. The strongest thematic overlap is: agentic workflows, AI content/video, SEO/AEO visibility, social/content automation, and monitoring/intelligence dashboards.

Cross-reference analysis

Exact overlap: Trendshift AND TrustMRR

None detected by exact project/startup name in the top fetched lists.

Strong thematic overlap: “wow + money” categories

Theme Trendshift signal TrustMRR validation Why it matters
AI agents as workflows superpowers, Claude-Code-Game-Studios, agency-agents, hermes-agent, cybersecurity skills SEOBOT, Postiz, DM Champ, PROSP Open-source hype is around agent kits; revenue is around agents that own a specific business workflow.
AI video/content generation hyperframes, Pixelle-Video, modly Vid.AI, Speel.co below top 20, creator/social products Content automation is moving from demos to revenue. The winning angle is distribution-ready output, not just generation.
Monitoring/intelligence worldmonitor, TrendRadar Cometly, SEOBOT/AEO-adjacent products Users pay when monitoring connects to decisions: ads, SEO, alerts, geopolitical/business intel.
SEO/AEO visibility TrendRadar and agent/content toolchains SEOBOT, SEO STACK, AEO Engine, Launch Club, LocalRank AI search has created a fresh budget line: “be visible in Google + ChatGPT + Perplexity.”
Creator/business-in-a-box agency-agents, design-md, video engines Stan, Supliful, Vid.AI, Postiz, Supergrow Creators pay for systems that compress production, storefront, distribution, and monetization.

Top 5 “Wow + Money” overlap projects/themes

  1. Postiz + Trendshift’s agent/social automation wave — Postiz is already at $97k MRR, +18%, while GitHub is excited about agentic workflows and content automation. Winning because scheduling becomes autonomous workflow execution.
  2. SEOBOT + agentic SEO/content tooling — $73.6k MRR, +8% validates that businesses pay for agents when tied to traffic/revenue.
  3. Vid.AI + hyperframes/Pixelle-Video — $94.2k MRR validates short-video automation. Trendshift shows developers want programmable/agent-ready video systems.
  4. Cometly + monitoring/intelligence dashboards — $212k MRR, +4% proves analytics sells when attached to ad spend. Trendshift’s worldmonitor/TrendRadar show broad appetite for intelligence surfaces.
  5. PROSP/DM Champ + agency-agents/sales-agent templates — $128k + $187k MRR validate AI outreach/sales agents. Open-source hype is the agent library; money is in narrow lead-to-cash automations.

Trendshift projects with zero or unclear monetization — opportunity list

  • andrej-karpathy-skills / superpowers / Claude-Code-Game-Studios / agency-agents / Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: package as paid templates, team training, managed workflow libraries, compliance packs.
  • TrendRadar / worldmonitor: hosted alerting, team workspaces, source connectors, enterprise monitoring.
  • awesome-design-md: paid premium brand packs, Figma/design-system export, team governance.
  • hyperframes / Pixelle-Video / modly: managed rendering, asset hosting, commercial licenses, creator SaaS.
  • awesome-free-llm-apis: developer lead-gen, affiliate/API marketplace, uptime/price monitoring.

TrustMRR money-makers with low visible GitHub hype — hidden gems

  • Stan — $3.57M MRR: creator commerce dwarfs the rest; not GitHub-hyped because buyer is non-technical.
  • TrimRx — $309.9k MRR, +11%: telehealth + GLP-1 demand continues printing.
  • Kibu — $234.3k MRR: vertical healthcare/compliance SaaS; boring, sticky, defensible.
  • Rezi — $285.4k MRR: resume/job-seeker market still monetizes at scale.
  • Cometly — $212.2k MRR: attribution remains budget-critical despite low open-source excitement.

Conclusions

1. Top trends

  • Agent hype is everywhere, but revenue comes from narrow business outcomes. Generic agent frameworks trend; specialized agents for SEO, sales, social scheduling, and ads analytics earn.
  • AI video is becoming infrastructure. Developers want programmable video rendering; customers pay for “idea/script → ready-to-post.”
  • AEO/SEO is a hot commercial wedge. TrustMRR shows multiple revenue businesses around AI/search visibility; Trendshift shows enabling tooling and monitoring interest.
  • Vertical SaaS still quietly wins. Kibu, TrimRx, and medical consulting are not flashy but solve regulated, painful problems.
  • Creator economy remains the biggest money pool. Stan’s $3.57M MRR is the outlier that proves creator monetization infrastructure is still massive.

2. Money vs hype gap

  • Hyped but not monetizing: agent skill packs, prompt/system-prompt collections, design prompt libraries, curated AI resources. These gain stars because they are immediately useful and free, but they need packaging, hosting, workflow management, or enterprise controls to monetize.
  • Quietly printing money: healthcare workflows, creator commerce, resume/career tools, attribution, LinkedIn outreach, and vertical compliance. These products are less exciting to developers but directly connected to revenue, health, jobs, or regulatory burden.

3. Opportunities

  • Hosted “agent skills registry” for teams: permissions, versioning, quality scoring, workflow packs, compliance mappings.
  • AEO/LLM visibility operations suite: monitor mentions across ChatGPT/Perplexity/Google AI Overviews, suggest content/actions, push tasks to agents.
  • Agent-ready video production API: HTML/script input, brand templates, rendered outputs, social publishing hooks.
  • Vertical agent wrappers: take generic open-source agent frameworks and specialize them for dentists, clinics, real estate teams, recruiting, local services.
  • Trend intelligence for operators: combine TrendRadar/worldmonitor-style feeds with business actions: “what should I ship/post/buy/watch today?”

4. Quick takes

  • Don’t build another generic agent framework unless it owns a workflow. The market rewards “agent that sells/books/ranks/posts,” not “agent that agents.”
  • Boring vertical SaaS beats flashy OSS on cash. Kibu and TrimRx have more monetization clarity than most starred repos.
  • AI video + distribution is the near-term wedge. Generation alone is becoming commodity; scheduling, brand control, analytics, and conversion are where margin lives.
